items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Return of the mums, retired shooter winds back the clock and Kiwis make hot start at Firebirds
items.summary: The world's best goal keeper has sparked an important conversation about postpartum depression in her return to the Super Netball league.

items.title: Matildas duo Hayley Raso and Steph Catley fit to play China in Asian Cup semifinal
items.summary: Hayley Raso and Steph Catley are available for selection, but it remains to be seen what role they will have against China in Perth.

items.title: Fuel shortages spark WA farmers' ire amid claims city is getting priority access
items.summary: Farmers welcome a rule change that allows road trains to deliver more fuel to regional areas, but say it does little to address the overall supply problem.

items.title: Former Queensland State of Origin player Chris Walker granted bail on DV charges
items.summary: Christopher Walker was arrested after he was allegedly found hiding in the backyard of a Gold Coast home.

items.title: AFL referred to antisemitism royal commission after opening round speech controversy
items.summary: The club says it takes full responsibility for a script change that removed references to the Jewish community during an opening round tribute to Bondi victims.

items.title: Homicide squad investigating death of man in Jung in western Victoria
items.summary: Homicide squad detectives are investigating the death of a man whose body was found in the western Victorian town of Jung.

items.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas doubles down on algal bloom health messaging
items.summary: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has doubled down on public health messaging about the state's algal bloom crisis saying the word "toxic" could also be used for alcohol. It follows questions raised by the ABC's Four Corner's program about the time frame in delivering health advice on the bloom.

items.title: Every NSW waterway contaminated with microplastics, study finds
items.summary: A NSW government study finds microplastics in all 120 estuaries sampled, with severe pollution in about 19 per cent of rivers and estuaries.

items.title: Magpies to challenge Scott Pendlebury's AFL ban, Swans star Errol Gulden out for four months
items.summary: Collingwood decides if it will contest Scott Pendlebury's one-match suspension at the AFL tribunal, as the veteran midfielder faces a ban for the first time in his 427-match career.

items.title: Four slow-cooker meals for cooler autumn evenings
items.summary: We've pulled together four lovely recipes that make weeknight meals a little easier (and quite budget friendly, too).

items.title: Oscars 2026 key moments: Australians miss out across all major categories
items.summary: Australians have missed out in every key category at the 98th Academy Awards. Michael B Jordan received a standing ovation when named Best Actor for Sinners. And Conan O'Brien also "wins". Here are your Oscars key moments.

items.title: Trial of driver in crash that killed four people near Shepparton to go ahead
items.summary: A judge refuses a request from defence lawyers to throw out charges regarding a fatal crash near Shepparton.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O network slapped with new licence conditions
items.summary: The media watchdog imposes fresh conditions on KIIS FM owner ARN Media, in response to repeated breaches by controversial radio show The Kyle & Jackie O Show.

items.title: Three new rock monitor lizard species discovered in Far North Queensland savanna
items.summary: The unexpected findings in the savanna country of north Queensland have changed scientists' understanding of rock monitors, but there are concerns the new species could be vulnerable to the pet trade.

items.title: David Speirs looking to overcome drugs sentence and return to South Australian parliament
items.summary: One-time Liberal opposition leader David Speirs is attempting a political comeback, but even he concedes it's a long shot after a very public downfall where he was convicted of supplying cocaine just over 10 months ago.

items.title: Hobart woman Karen Blake pleads guilty to 21 animal cruelty charges after animals seized from property
items.summary: A Tasmanian woman has pleaded guilty to 21 animal cruelty charges. More than 70 animals were found living in "squalid" conditions, with some requiring euthanasing.

items.title: Gone in 15 seconds: thousands of Victorian cars stolen using high-tech devices
items.summary: Police say criminals are able to steal certain push-button start models within 15 seconds, using key reprogramming devices bought online.

items.summary: The King and Queen of Denmark will visit the queen's home town of Hobart on the last day of their official visit to Australia.

items.title: Court told mother faked son's cancer diagnosis to cover her overspending
items.summary: An Adelaide mother who faked her six-year-old son's cancer diagnosis to deceitfully raise money was "living the life of the rich and famous" despite serious financial struggles, a court has heard.

items.title: Danish King Frederik and Queen Mary honour fallen, meet the public at the Australian War Memorial
items.summary: A crowd of people waving Danish flags waited outside the Australian War Memorial for a chance to meet King Frederick and Queen Mary on day three of their royal tour.

items.title: Victorian government agrees to $125 million COVID hotel quarantine class action settlement
items.summary: The state government has agreed to a $125 million settlement, which has to be approved by the Supreme Court.

items.title: Australian author Craig Silvey faces further child exploitation charges
items.summary: Popular Australian author Craig Silvey is charged with two more child exploitation offences, including allegedly producing child exploitation material.

items.summary: The family of a six-year-old girl with autism who fell from the cliffs at a popular NSW South Coast lookout a year ago want to see more inclusive public spaces.

items.title: Oscars 2026 winners list: Michael B Jordan takes Best Actor, Jessie Buckley best actress
items.summary: Here's the updated winners list as the ceremony takes place.

items.title: Fish price spike, supply issues months after WA's demersal fishing ban
items.summary: The ABC has spoken to six fishmongers who have had to increase prices after the WA government's demersal fishing ban, with consumers set to pay 10 to 40 per cent more for most species this Easter.

items.title: Kimberley farmers face three-year wait for new weather radar
items.summary: Farmers in WA's north typically rely on real-time radar data for planting purposes during the wet season.

items.title: Oscars 2026 red carpet: Rose Byrne, Nicole Kidman represent Australia on Academy Awards red carpet
items.summary: Before the ceremony of the 98th Oscars is the spectacle that is the red carpet. See all the most striking looks here.

items.title: Homeless Indigenous elder died after years on 'priority' WA public housing waitlist
items.summary: The Wongatha man from WA's Goldfields died in Kalgoorlie-Boulder, about 600km east of Perth, on February 6 after battling emphysema and lung cancer.

items.title: Queensland arts minister approved name for Glasshouse Theatre before public vote opened, documents show
items.summary: Documents released under right to information show Queensland's arts minister was "set" on the name in January, months before the community was invited to have their say.

items.title: Rose Byrne, Jacob Elordi miss out at Oscars as One Battle After Another dominates — as it happened
items.summary: Australians Rose Byrne and Jacob Elordi miss out on their first Oscars, as Michael B Jordan gets a standing ovation for winning Best Actor and One Battle After Another sweeps the field.

items.title: Impassioned plea from Tasmania's top cop no match for political realities on gun ownership caps
items.summary: Gun law reform is always fraught, especially in Tasmania. An impassioned plea from the state's top cop hasn't been enough to sway the government on gun ownership caps, but opposition and crossbench MPs may be able to pass the limits into law.

items.title: UEFA says intercontinental football champions match between Argentina and Spain is off due to Middle East war
items.summary: A meeting of men's football's South American and European champions — the Finalissima — featuring superstars Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal, has been called off due to security concerns amid the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Three New South Wales fishermen found dead off SA's Beachport
items.summary: SA police say the alarm was raised yesterday afternoon, when the three men did not return to shore at Beachport in the state's south-east. A local fisher said he tried to warn the men before they left that conditions were rough.

items.title: ASX slips as war rattles global stocks, RBA rate hike looms — as it happened
items.summary: Global investors are still rotating out of shares as the Iranian conflict continues and rising oil prices stoke inflation fears, but locally the ASX has finished down just 0.4pc. Look back at how the trading session unfolded.

items.title: Popular mango farm forced to sell as compliance measures pressure producers
items.summary: Mango industry representatives say addressing mounting compliance requirements is like "trying to turn back the Titanic".

items.title: Fifth member of Iranian women's soccer team leaves Australia
items.summary: A fifth member of the Iranian women's football team withdrew her asylum claim and left the country overnight, the federal government says.

items.title: Nepali security guard killed in Abu Dhabi shows risks faced by 'trapped' migrant workers
items.summary: The killing of a Nepali security guard in Abu Dhabi highlights the growing risks faced by millions of migrant workers across the Gulf as the conflict between Iran, Israel and the United States escalates.

items.title: Fuel rationing a chance in Australia if war continues to trim global oil supplies, experts say
items.summary: Experts say Australians should prepare for the possibility of widespread fuel rationing if the war in the Middle East is prolonged.

items.title: Study of Brisbane family helps researchers uncover genetic mutation linked to ulcerative colitis
items.summary: Researchers have used advanced genetic sequencing on a Brisbane family to identify a gene mutation linked to ulcerative colitis, a type of inflammatory bowel condition, sparking hopes of new treatments.

items.title: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce predicts party will 'poll number one' in many seats at SA election
items.summary: Federal MP Barnaby Joyce is predicting a big primary vote for One Nation at this Saturday's South Australian election, but lead candidate Cory Bernardi would not be drawn on how many lower house seats he hoped the party would win.

items.title: What is lifestyle creep? Here's why it happens and how to avoid it
items.summary: If you tend to splurge on more indulgences any time you receive a pay rise, you may be suffering from lifestyle creep — and it could have a longstanding effect on your finances.

items.title: Australia's refugee policy is tested as Iran's humanitarian crisis expands
items.summary: Does the same principle that led Australia to support Israel and the US in Iran also extend to offering more humanitarian assistance?

items.title: Doctors want better protection for workers exposed to workplace chemicals labelled 'silent killers'
items.summary: Under the proposed changes, there would be a five-fold reduction in the exposure limit for benzene, chlorine exposure would be reduced ten-fold, and silica exposure would be halved.

items.title: What the government knew about SA’s toxic algal bloom and what it told the public
items.summary: The government knew a harmful toxin had been found in its algal bloom, but took months to update its health advice.

items.title: These musicians stole our hearts on the first season of The Piano, so what are they up to now?
items.summary: In the first season of The Piano, nobody quite knew what to expect. But what happens after you get the chance to play for a national audience? We catch up with some of the pianists to find out how music continues to shape their lives.

items.title: This week in history quiz: COVID cruise ships, Neighbours launches and walking in space
items.summary: This week in years past saw the first ever spacewalk, the Harbour Bridge opening and more importantly the premiere of Neighbours. Test your memory.

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan describe new book on royal family as 'deranged conspiracy'
items.summary: Extracts of the new book, including a section saying Queen Camilla once told a friend Meghan had "brainwashed" her husband, have been published in an English newspaper.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Japan dominates Philippines to cruise into semifinals and delight diaspora
items.summary: Sayoko had never played football, but the Japan women's side inspired her to take up the game. And the Nadeshiko lived up to the hype at Stadium Australia, defeating the Philippines 7-0 on an afternoon of colour and national pride.

items.title: Danish King Frederik and Queen Mary visit governor-general's house in Canberra as royal tour continues
items.summary: Danish Queen Mary speaks of the similarities between the two countries as she and her husband King Frederik arrive in Canberra on day two of their Australian tour.

items.title: Three fishermen missing off Beachport along SA's Limestone Coast
items.summary: Emergency crews are responding after three fishermen were declared missing at Beachport in South Australia's south-east.

items.summary: Raissa Butkowski has been selected as the Liberal candidate for the seat of Farrer.

items.title: North-west town the first in Victoria to run out of fuel
items.summary: Robinvale, located about 70 kilometres east from Mildura, ran out of fuel on Saturday evening.

items.title: 'Half their salary cap gone': Knights count cost of win over Manly
items.summary: Kalyn Ponga and Dylan Brown are both forced from the field in the first half as the Newcastle Knights ran riot against the Sea Eagles at Brookvale Oval, before the Dolphins storm back from 14-0 down to beat the Titans and become the first Queensland team to win a game this season.

items.title: France wins back-to-back Six Nations Championships, clinching the title with a 48-46 win over England
items.summary: After five rounds of matches, the Six Nations Championship comes down to a single penalty in the 83rd minute of the final game — and France emerges as back-to-back champions.
